D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Cardinal Red — Vatican Cassock Protocol

Pope Paul II in 1464 swapped cardinals from Tyrian purple to crimson kermes after the fall of Constantinople cut off Murex supply; the symbolism became "willing to shed blood for Christ"

Catholic Church liturgical colour code: white (Easter, Christmas), red (Pentecost, martyrs), green (ordinary time), violet (Lent, Advent), rose (Gaudete/Laetare). Cardinals wear scarlet; bishops violet; pope white; deacons varies. Sacred symbolism mirrors political signalling.

Toggle Church rank and current liturgical season.
